title: ‘The King of Staten Island’ and the Judd Apatow Hall of Fame
format: Sean and Amanda review Pete Davidson's VOD dramedy, then spend the back half walking Judd Apatow's entire career to seat a 10-slot Hall of Fame.

segments.heading: ‘The King of Staten Island’
segments.note: Apatow's Pete Davidson movie, straight to VOD

films.title: The King of Staten Island
films.note: Sean liked it more than the last few Apatow movies; Amanda watched it like a 10-episode TV dramedy squeezed into two hours. Shot by Robert Elswit (several Paul Thomas Anderson films, Michael Clayton), which both of them hear in how differently it's lit from Apatow's earlier work. Runs 2:17 and doesn't quite need it, but the Bill Burr performance surprised them both.

segments.heading: Reference points
segments.note: the movies that came up while placing it

films.title: Being There
films.note: Amanda's comp for the Hal Ashby-style movie Apatow keeps reaching for but hasn't quite made yet.

films.title: Set It Up
films.note: Pete Davidson's scene-stealing bit part, cited as the template for how he tends to get used outside his own projects.

segments.heading: The Judd Apatow Hall of Fame
segments.note: movies only (the TV picks that made the show's actual list are noted below)

films.title: Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy
films.note: Left out early for being 'a different school' of comedy, then reinstated at the very end over The King of Staten Island: Sean's pick over the new movie.

films.title: The 40-Year-Old Virgin
films.note: Apatow's directing debut, built around Steve Carell after Anchorman. Unanimous yes.

films.title: Knocked Up
films.note: Made $220 million and launched the whole guys-riffing-in-a-room era. In, despite Amanda's reservations about how thin the Katherine Heigl character is.

films.title: Superbad
films.note: Apatow only produced, but by both accounts he's the reason it got made. Called the most beloved movie on the list.

films.title: Step Brothers
films.note: Added as the Ferrell-McKay slot after passing on Anchorman earlier in the count, a placeholder pick that stuck.

films.title: Funny People
films.note: Sean's pick for Apatow's best movie, despite the Eric Bana material in the back half. Amanda agrees the first 90 minutes are the strongest thing on the list.

films.title: Bridesmaids
films.note: Apatow pushed for the bridal-shop gross-out scene himself. In for what the movie did for comedies built around women, even with reservations about needing that scene to sell it.

films.title: The Big Sick
films.note: Produced, not directed. Kumail Nanjiani and Emily Gordon's script. Amanda's most personal pick on the list.

segments.heading: The rest of the vault
segments.note: considered and cut from the final ten

films.title: Heavyweights
films.note: Ben Stiller movie about a fat camp that Apatow cites as a formative failure. Passed.

films.title: Celtic Pride
films.note: Damon Wayans heist comedy, called maybe the worst movie Apatow has ever worked on. Passed.

films.title: The Cable Guy
films.note: Sean argued to induct it early, then walked it back before the final count. Out.

films.title: Talladega Nights: The Ballad of Ricky Bobby
films.note: Passed alongside Anchorman, as more of the same entry.

films.title: Walk Hard: The Dewey Cox Story
films.note: Apatow wrote or conceived the story. Liked, not inducted.

films.title: Forgetting Sarah Marshall
films.note: A Ringer favorite, still left out.

films.title: Pineapple Express
films.note: Big cult following that neither host feels strongly about anymore.

films.title: You Don't Mess with the Zohan
films.note: Second-tier Sandler, per Amanda. Not in contention.

films.title: Year One
films.note: One of the more maligned movies on Apatow's résumé. No debate.

films.title: Get Him to the Greek
films.note: Named and dismissed in the same breath: only Bridesmaids survives from this stretch of the catalog.

films.title: Wanderlust
films.note: Same fate as Get Him to the Greek.

films.title: The Five-Year Engagement
films.note: Same.

films.title: This Is 40
films.note: Sean's least favorite Apatow movie, too close to his own life for either host's comfort.

films.title: Anchorman 2: The Legend Continues
films.note: Ruled out as a package deal with the first Anchorman, before Sean reversed himself on the original.

films.title: Trainwreck
films.note: The inflection point where, per Sean, 'the magic ran out on his strategy': Amy Schumer both playing and subverting the Goldie Hawn rom-com lead. Both like it more than its reputation suggests. Still out.

films.title: Doc & Darryl
films.note: Apatow co-directed this Doc Gooden/Darryl Strawberry documentary. Personal for Sean, a fellow Mets fan, but he keeps it out given how much of Gooden's addiction they couldn't address directly.

films.title: May It Last: A Portrait of the Avett Brothers
films.note: Amanda hasn't seen it; neither host makes a case for it.

TV (discussed, not inducted): The Zen Diaries of Garry Shandling

Talladega Nights: The Ballad of Ricky Bo.title: Talladega Nights: The Ballad of Ricky Bobby
